text: Now that the mcp has been restarted, why don't we test out the shape of it? There's another open bug, so Im asking you to solve it, but also to analyze how the new tools function. Try your best to use the new workflow

You have a great context on the issue, so you should run those final two tasks of the chain. Please also keep in mind: during this chain we retired our hooks to remind you to file friction or to the vault, so AC also includes you being drawn to take those actions at the appropriate times

I need to send you a bunch of context from a crashed conversation. Everything that follows is a conversation: Chain closed cleanly. reference-resolution-migration CLOSED 2026-05-19 00:21. Three commits this session: 128f793, b3ee6c4, 1141452. T13 post-migration report card. Surprising finding: sub-agent admin.server_version returned git_sha=715dc19 while HEAD=b3ee6c4 stdio binary 5 commits behind. Strongest argument for SessionStart staleness signal. Friction + vault filed: 2 bugs parse-context-token-can-appear-in-references-and-no-hit-tokens, parse-context-first-call-skill-lacks-retry-on-error-fallback-rule; 1 vault decision 2026-05-18_retire-reminder-hooks-for-substrate-mediated-discovery.md; 1 project memory project_parse_context_directive_state_gap.md. User chose Flavor A SessionStart hook informational implementation. #5 framing: parse_context should always give agent what it needs across ~12 surfaces of index (task, bug, skill, vault, etc.), load agent as lean as possible, low confidence responses point toward kiwix destinations.

Fix bug 1458 (roadmap-list-undocumented-default-scope-filter) end-to-end. admin.action_describe(surface='work', action='roadmap_list') returns only {purpose: ...} with no params field. Calls without params return positions 5-13 (open only); positions 1-4 don't appear. Fix candidates: (a) populate roadmap_list's action_describe with params + default-value docs; (b) make the default return ALL entries. Steps: read bug body, investigate go/internal/work/roadmap.go::HandleRoadmapList and go/internal/work/actions_discovery.go, pick fix, run scripts/precommit.sh, commit, bug_resolve.

In our frontend, when I look at chains % tasks, bug index, suggestion index, I can see a similar pattern across the board. There's a header, a refresh button, then sort/filter/search options available depending on the page. However, I can see that on bug and suggestion index, it renders on 2 lines, where in chains and tasks index, it renders single-line. What is this the case?
can you please fix that bug? Also, right before you start, let me know if this prompt successfully handed you the bug fixing discipline info

There are three folders at /home/user/Documents/files which I've been using as a sort of pre-chain pipeline. It's a helpful arrangement while all of our critical migrations are happening so that the db and frontend dont get overpopulated while things land and settle. The three folders are already-processed ideas (most of these became chains, many are closed already), ideas-to-process, which is where ideas end up as single md docs when they are worthy of becoming one or more chains, but don't belong in the db yet, and intake-content, where I use general ideas.md to just dump all my random thoughts, and mining opportunities, where we learn from open source projects to improve our own methodologies. Can you read those over, make sure you understand, maybe write a few vault filings/vault memories so that you remember this structure, maybe even some vault memories to connect already-processed-ideas to the work that emerged from them. Take as long as you need, please make sure you batch where you can
